title: End of module: Kinematics — Constant Acceleration

questions.text: A car has initial velocity $u = 8\,\text{m s}^{-1}$ and accelerates uniformly at $a = 2\,\text{m s}^{-2}$ for $t = 5\,\text{s}$. Find its final velocity $v$.
A particle starts from rest ($u = 0$) and accelerates uniformly at $a = 3\,\text{m s}^{-2}$. How far does it travel in $t = 4\,\text{s}$?
A train starts from rest and accelerates uniformly at $a = 2\,\text{m s}^{-2}$ over a distance of $s = 100\,\text{m}$. Find its final speed $v$.
questions.explanation: Time is neither given nor wanted, so use $v^2 = u^2 + 2as$: $v^2 = 0 + 2 \times 2 \times 100 = 400$, hence $v = \sqrt{400} = 20\,\text{m s}^{-1}$.

questions.text: A stone is dropped from rest and falls freely under gravity ($g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$). What is its speed after $t = 3\,\text{s}$?
questions.explanation: Taking down as positive with $u = 0$ and $a = g$, use $v = u + at$: $v = 0 + 9.81 \times 3 = 29.43\,\text{m s}^{-1}$.

questions.text: An object is dropped from rest. Using $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$, how far does it fall in the first $t = 2\,\text{s}$?
A ball is thrown vertically upwards with initial speed $u = 19.62\,\text{m s}^{-1}$ ($g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$). How long (in seconds) until it returns to its starting height?
questions.explanation: By symmetry the total flight time is $t = \dfrac{2u}{g}$ (the time up equals the time down). So $t = \dfrac{2 \times 19.62}{9.81} = \dfrac{39.24}{9.81} = 4\,\text{s}$.

questions.text: A ball is thrown vertically upwards at $u = 19.62\,\text{m s}^{-1}$ ($g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$). What maximum height does it reach?
questions.explanation: At the top $v = 0$. Use $v^2 = u^2 - 2gH$, so $0 = u^2 - 2gH$ giving $H = \dfrac{u^2}{2g} = \dfrac{19.62^2}{2 \times 9.81} = \dfrac{384.94}{19.62} = 19.62\,\text{m}$.

questions.text: On a velocity-time graph, what does the gradient (slope) of the line represent?
questions.explanation: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity, $a = \dfrac{\mathrm{d}v}{\mathrm{d}t}$, which is exactly the gradient of a velocity-time graph.

questions.text: On a velocity-time graph, what does the area between the line and the time axis represent?
questions.explanation: Velocity multiplied by time gives displacement, so the area under a velocity-time graph is the displacement, $s = \int v\,\mathrm{d}t$.

questions.text: For a projectile moving under gravity alone (no air resistance), what is its horizontal acceleration?
questions.explanation: There is no horizontal force, so the horizontal acceleration is $0$ and the horizontal velocity $u_x = u\cos\theta$ stays constant. Gravity acts only vertically.

questions.text: Ignoring air resistance, which launch angle gives a projectile its maximum range over level ground?
questions.explanation: The range is $R = \dfrac{u^2 \sin 2\theta}{g}$, which is largest when $\sin 2\theta = 1$, i.e. $2\theta = 90^\circ$, so $\theta = 45^\circ$.
